key_evidence: UN Special Procedures / OHCHR (Feb 2026, reiterated Jun 2026): enforced disappearances by Malian government forces and the 'Wagner group' follow a systematic pattern that 'may constitute crimes against humanity' — state-perpetrator conduct.
Within-window (May 8-11, 2026): three political opposition leaders / junta critics disappeared in Bamako, extending the documented pattern.
Mali still carries a uniform-1.5 (12.5) baseline that has NOT been reweighted for active state perpetration, unlike peer Burkina Faso (reweighted to 6.3 on 2026-06-01) and India (15.6).

Cross-peer calibration concern: Mali (12.5) sits ABOVE Burkina Faso (6.3) despite a comparable UN-documented enforced-disappearance pattern. Per §3e-bis(5), this is a calibration matter for coordinator-level review, not an assessor one-off reset. Carried from 2026-06-24, still open.

evidence.quote: Enforced disappearances follow a pattern in Mali. The frequency of the practice, its organised nature and methods used indicate a systematic character
evidence.claim: UN experts find Mali's enforced disappearances are systematic, a threshold element of crimes against humanity

evidence.quote: OHCHR documented hundreds of extrajudicial executions, arbitrary arrests and detentions and enforced disappearances by all parties
evidence.claim: OHCHR documents extrajudicial executions and enforced disappearances in Mali including by government-aligned forces

note: REFRESHED 2026-06-25 (carried from 2026-06-24, still open). NOT a score-move proposal. Mali's uniform-1.5 baseline appears under-calibrated relative to reweighted Sahel peers given UN-documented systematic enforced disappearances by state forces + Wagner ('may constitute CAH', pre-adjudication), now extended by the May 8-11 Bamako opposition disappearances. Per §3e-bis(5), routed as a calibration flag for coordinator-level ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT review across the Sahel band (Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger/Chad context), NOT as an assessor one-off downgrade. Confirmed at 12.5 pending that review.
triage_note: Triage 2026-07-21: superseded by newer assessment 2026-07-14. Not applied; current published score is authoritative.
